WORK EXPERIENCE

Dyson
Hullavington, UK
Senior Robotics Research Control Engineer
Dec 2022 – present
  • Research: highly nonlinear system control using optimal control and reinforcement learning
  • Development: hardware agonistic robot software framework
SLMC Laboratory at University of Edinburgh
Edinburgh, UK
Laboratory Robotics Software Engineer
Jun 2021 – Sep 2022
  • Research: footstep planning and control parameterization for full-body dynamics MPC for quadrupeds
  • Development: software framework, feedforward PD controller, Vicon state estimator for legged robots
  • Collaboration: perceptive locomotion for quadrupeds, centroidal dynamics footstep planner for humanoids
  • Management: robot installation, documentation for robot usage, and setup
  • Teaching: robot programming and RobOps to Ph.D. students and postdocs
  • Software: C++, Python, Linux, ROS, ROS2, RViz, Gazebo, Pybullet, Git
  • Robot: ANYbotics ANYmal, Pal Robotics Talos, Kawada Nextage, ODRI Solo, Vicon
ROBOTIS
Seoul, Korea
Senior Robotics Research Engineer
Jan 2021 – Jun 2021
Robotics Research Engineer
Apr 2018 – Jan 2021
  • Development: software framework, graphical user interfaces for wheeled, arm, and legged robots, wheeled robots that navigate and ride elevators, forward and inverse kinematics for serial and parallel manipulator structures
  • Software: C++, Python, Matlab, Linux, ROS, ROS2, RViz, Gazebo, Qt, Git, Travis, Docker, Onshape
  • Library: ROS Control, Navigation Stack, MoveIt, YOLO, KERAS
  • Design: customizable small-sized serial and parallel manipulators
  • Robot: ZIPGAEMI, Turtlebot3, OpenManipulator, RH Gripper, small-sized ROBOTIS humanoid
  • OpenSource: open-source robot development and maintenance, communication with the ROS community
CARIS Laboratory at University of British Columbia
Vancouver, BC, Canada
Research Assistant
Sep 2015 – Dec 2017
  • Research: haptic cues in bimanual cooperative transport of large objects for future human-robot interaction
  • Development: analysis of numerical haptic data and modeling of human arm impedance
  • Software: Matlab, C++ (Arduino)
  • Design: human subject experiment, mechanical and electrical design for experimental tools, sensor calibration
